metadata.description: Microsoft Sentinel Analytics Rule: Brute Force Detection
metadata.nistControls: AU-6 (audit review), SI-4 (monitoring), AC-7 (unsuccessful login attempts)
metadata.author: JSA AppSec
metadata.WHY: Failed login spikes from a single IP are the clearest signal of a brute-force or credential-stuffing attack. 5 failures in 5 minutes is low noise and high signal. This rule fires before the attacker succeeds.

metadata.description: The name of the Log Analytics workspace (Sentinel)

properties.displayName: Brute Force — Multiple Failed Sign-Ins from Single IP
properties.description: Detects 5 or more failed Azure AD sign-in attempts from a single IP address within 5 minutes. Indicates brute force or credential stuffing. NIST AU-6, AC-7. MITRE T1110.
properties.query:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| where ResultType != "0"
| where ResultType !in ("50140", "50173")
| summarize
    FailedAttempts    = count(),
    TargetAccounts    = make_set(UserPrincipalName),
    ErrorCodes        = make_set(ResultType),
    FirstSeen         = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen          = max(TimeGenerated)
    by IPAddress,
       Location       = tostring(LocationDetails.city),
       Country        = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| where FailedAttempts >= 5
| extend
    AccountCount      = array_length(TargetAccounts),
    Duration          = LastSeen - FirstSeen
